headline: Intel rises after Musk signals use of 14A process for Tesla-linked Terafab
summary: Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) shares moved higher in premarket trading on Thursday after Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk indicated that the Terafab venture plans to adopt Intel’s advanced 14A semiconductor manufacturing process. Speaking during Tesla’s post-earnings call, Musk said the 14A node is “not yet totally complete,” but should be “probably fairly mature or ready for prime time” by the time Terafab reaches scale.

content.title: Intel rises after Musk signals use of 14A process for Tesla-linked Terafab
content.summary: Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) shares moved higher in premarket trading on Thursday after Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk indicated that the Terafab venture plans to adopt Intel’s advanced 14A semiconductor manufacturing process. Speaking during Tesla’s post-earnings call, Musk said the 14A node is “not yet totally complete,” but should be “probably fairly mature or ready for prime time” by the time Terafab reaches scale.

headline: Intel forecasts second-quarter revenue above estimates, shares jump 15%
summary: Intel forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations on Thursday, underscoring booming demand for the company's server processors used for artificial intelligence in data centers.  Shares of Intel surged 15% ‌in extended trading, adding $49 billion to its market value and extending its 81% rebound so far this year.  After years of management blunders left the former icon of chipmaking without a meaningful ​foothold in the booming artificial-intelligence industry, CEO Lip-Bu Tan put a revival plan in place to shore up Intel's balance sheet through asset sales and layoffs.

content.title: Intel forecasts second-quarter revenue above estimates, shares jump 15%
content.summary: Intel forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations on Thursday, underscoring booming demand for the company's server processors used for artificial intelligence in data centers.  Shares of Intel surged 15% ‌in extended trading, adding $49 billion to its market value and extending its 81% rebound so far this year.  After years of management blunders left the former icon of chipmaking without a meaningful ​foothold in the booming artificial-intelligence industry, CEO Lip-Bu Tan put a revival plan in place to shore up Intel's balance sheet through asset sales and layoffs.

headline: Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) Delivers Strong Q1 Numbers, Stock Jumps 12.7%
summary: Computer processor maker Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 7.2% year on year to $13.58 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($14.3 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 9.2% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.29 per share was significantly above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) Delivers Strong Q1 Numbers, Stock Jumps 12.7%
content.summary: Computer processor maker Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 7.2% year on year to $13.58 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($14.3 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 9.2% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.29 per share was significantly above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Stock market today: Nasdaq futures climb as Intel soars, while Hormuz tensions keep markets on edge
content.summary: Investors are assessing Trump's announcement that Israel and Lebanon will extend their ceasefire.

headline: Intel shares surge 23% after earnings, guidance "significantly" beat expectations
summary: Investing.com -- Intel Corp on Thursday projected its second quarter revenue and profit well-above Wall Street estimates on strong demand for the chipmaker’s server chips used in artificial-intelligence data centers. The company also reported much better-than-expected first-quarter results.

The company also reported much better-than-expected first-quarter results.
